Dr. Jakob Thomae, co-founder and CEO of Theia Finance Labs, argues for the motion, while Dr. Ben Caldecott, founding director of the Oxford Sustainable Finance Group and a coordinating lead author on finance for the IPCC’s Seventh Assessment Report, argues against it. title: Nuclear Fusion Powers Up for Commercial Breakthrough (Podcast)
summary: Fusion energy is entering a new phase. After decades as a scientific pursuit, the sector is attracting billions in private and public capital, with big tech, oil companies and governments all racing to bring the first commercial plants online. Advances in areas like superconductors, materials and computing are opening new pathways, while surging demand for reliable, clean power, particularly from data centers, is accelerating momentum. But the landscape remains uncertain, with multiple competing

summary: The European Union proposed measures such as optimizing jet fuel distribution and cutting energy taxes to navigate the Iran war-driven crisis that it said could reverberate for years.

title: Coffee Climbs on Iran Conflict Concerns and Brazil Weather Risk
summary: Arabica coffee climbed the most in two weeks in New York, amid concerns that the Middle East conflict will keep logistics costs elevated and as weather threatens Brazilian crops.

summary: Trading firms have put the lessons of the 2022 energy crisis to good use during the Iran war.

title: Europe Power Markets See More Negative Prices as Solar Surges
summary: Europe’s power markets are seeing a sharp rise in negative electricity prices, particularly around midday when solar generation exceeds demand.

title: Philip Morris Smoke-Free Shift Gathers Pace as Profit Rises
summary: Philip Morris International Inc.’s profit rose more than expected in the first quarter on sales of smoke-free products including heated tobacco and nicotine pouches.

title: Iran Seizes Two Ships in Hormuz for ‘Violations’: Tasnim
summary: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had seized two ships in Hormuz and brought them to shore for inspection Wednesday, according to reports from Iranian state television. Tyler Kendall has more on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Blue Owl Capital, more than almost any other firm on Wall Street, needed a win.

title: Vista Strikes Deal to Speed Up Google AI in Software Portfolio
summary: Vista Equity Partners is partnering with Alphabet Inc.’s Google Cloud to accelerate the deployment of artificial intelligence across the private equity firm’s portfolio of more than 90 software firms.

title: Wall Street’s Bullish Stocks View Contrasts With Consumer Gloom
summary: Stocks are trading near a record high, signaling Wall Street is learning to cope with lingering geopolitical risks. Main Street is struggling to catch up.

title: ECB’s Kocher Says Too Early to Predict Next Week’s Rate Outcome
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Martin Kocher said uncertainty over the Iran war means he can’t yet predict what will happen when rate-setters meet in Frankfurt April 29-30.

title: Stocks Get Earnings Boost as Traders Focus on Iran
summary: Stocks rose amid strong corporate earnings as traders waited to see if Iran will join talks that could restore oil flows from the Middle East. The dollar edged higher while Treasuries slipped ahead of Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing.

title: Trump Extends Ceasefire; Users Get Unauthorized Access to Mythos | Bloomberg Brief 4/22/2026
summary: US equity futures rise and brent briefly hit $100 per barrel after President Trump indefinitely extends a ceasefire with Iran. Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing signals a complicated path to Fed Chair. A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic's new Mythos AI model. Lindsay Newman of Kings College London discusses the implications of the ceasefire extension. Kelsey Berro of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses the concerns around the Federal Reserve's independence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Toronto Exchange Parent to Buy Cboe’s Australia, Canada Units
summary: Toronto Stock Exchange owner TMX Group Ltd. is set to acquire Cboe Global Markets Inc.’s Australian and Canadian units.

title: Emerging Markets Rally Hits Pause as Iran Risks Lift Oil
summary: Revved-up emerging-market assets declined as rising oil prices and the lack of progress in Iran peace talks zapped risk appetite.

title: Druzhba Flows Resume as EU Targets Unblocking Ukraine Loan
summary: Oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline from Ukraine have resumed, Hungarian refiner Mol Nyrt. said, a development that’s expected to help unblock crucial European Union aid to Kyiv on Wednesday.

title: US Home-Purchase Applications Jump as Mortgage Rates Settle Back
summary: US mortgage applications for home purchases increased last week by the most since early January as financing costs continued to ease, a tentative sign that the housing market is grinding forward.

title: Private Credit BDCs’ 2028 Maturity Wall Poses Risk, Moody’s Says
summary: Private credit funds with outsized exposure to software and tech loans face rising refinancing and credit risks as a wave of debt maturities loom from 2028, according to Moody’s Ratings.

title: Gold Recovers After Two-Day Drop as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire
summary: Gold regained some ground after a two-day decline, as US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and allowed more time to arrange fresh peace talks.

title: EQT Chief Sees AI Rout as Opportunity for Fresh Tech Bets
summary: The broad selloff in public markets triggered by fears of artificial intelligence disruption provides an opportunity to snap up technology firms on the cheap, according to private equity giant EQT AB.

title: Iran Latest: Trump Backtracks & Extends Tehran Truce, Talks in Limbo | The Opening Trade 4/22/2026
summary:
US President Donald Trump indefinitely extended a ceasefire with Iran as peace talks remain on hold, walking back threats to resume fighting even as the Strait of Hormuz remains all but shut.

Trump said Pakistan, the main mediator between the warring sides, asked the US to hold off on fresh strikes, something Tehran denied was the case. Washington is extending the truce — which began just over two weeks ago — until Iran submits a new proposal “and discussions are concluded, one way or the other,” Trump said on Truth Social late on Tuesday.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dell’s XPS 16 Laptop Marks a Return to Form After PC Business Got ‘Off Course’
summary: It’s not unheard of for tech companies to reverse a big marketing decision, but few have done so as quickly as Dell Technologies Inc. In early 2025, the company announced it would move away from its long-running XPS laptop branding in favor of simpler naming schemes like “Pro” and “Max” intended to appeal to a broader market of consumers accustomed to Apple Inc.’s naming strategy. By the following January, Dell realized it had made a mistake and quickly pivoted back.

title: Chip Bonus Boom: How Korea Learned Everyone Cares About Other People’s Money
summary: Samsung workers press chipmaker to follow SK Hynix example and share massive profits

title: AT&T Beats Estimates Buoyed by Internet, Phone Subscribers
summary: AT&T Inc. reported revenue and profits in the first quarter that beat analysts’ estimates, as the No. 3 US wireless carrier continued to expand its fiber footprint and sell customers on its convergence strategy of subscribing to multiple products at once.

title: Tesla’s Cooling AI Hype Overshadows Blowout Earnings Forecasts
summary: Tesla Inc. investors are in for a rare treat Wednesday afternoon: an earnings report that analysts say should be a blowout. The trouble is the actual numbers are likely to get overlooked as Wall Street seeks evidence that Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence and robotics ventures justify the stock’s sky-high valuation.

title: OpenAI Poaches Airbnb Executive to be First Director for EMEA
summary: OpenAI has hired its first managing director to oversee operations in Europe, helping guide its expansion in a key market where there are mounting political concerns about being overly reliant on US artificial intelligence services.

title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs Full Combination with T-Mobile
summary: Deutsche Telekom is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile, a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal. Manuel Baigorri reports on the Bloomberg scoop. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Naspers Mulls Options for South Africa’s Historic ‘City Press’
summary: Naspers Ltd., Africa’s largest company by market value, is exploring strategic options for City Press, according to people familiar with the matter, a historic newspaper in the continent’s biggest economy.

title: AI Startup Has Helped Reverse Thousands of Denied Health Insurance Claims
summary: Americans rarely fight back when insurers reject treatments their doctors have prescribed. Claimable is working to change that, with a little help from Mark Cuban.

title: Japan Top Officials to Meet Banks to Discuss Mythos Threat
summary: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said she will meet the country’s biggest banks to discuss Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos on Friday, as authorities around the world express concerns about the technology.

title: ASM Outlook Beats Estimates as AI Boom Drives Tool Demand
summary: ASM International NV projected revenue for the second quarter that exceeded analysts’ estimates, underscoring that artificial intelligence investments are driving demand for the Dutch chip-equipment maker’s gear.

title: Daniel Yergin Sees a 'Different World' Emerging After the Hormuz Crisis
summary: When it comes to the history of oil and energy, nobody is more famous or well respected than Daniel Yergin. He is the Vice Chairman of S&P Global, and the Pulitzer Prize winning author of both The Prize: The Epic Quest for Oil, Money, and Power and The New Map: Energy, Climate, and the Clash of Nations. So we had to get his insights on the war in Iran, and its historical significance. Iran's ability to control the Straight against a much stronger military is a demonstration that the balance of global power is changing, with profound ramifications for countries around the world. We discuss how different regions are being affected, and how it will change their calculus when it comes to energy security. We also talk about the AI industry's seemingly insatiable demand for electricity, and how this is rippling across the entire energy landscape.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Reports 'Some Sign' US Ready to Break Blockade | Daybreak Europe 4/22/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia.

On today's show, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim news reports that there's been 'some sign' that the US is ready to end its blockade of Iran's ports. Brent crude, the dollar and US Treasury yields all dipped on the report. The US President has extended the ceasefire with Iran indefinitely. Tehran has so far refused to take part in a second round of peace talks.

Bloomberg understands that unauthorized users have gained access to Mythos, Anthropic's most powerful, and potentially dangerous, AI model.

Plus Donald Trump's pick to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, has promised to be independent, telling the Senate Banking Committee that he won't be the President's sock puppet. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ABB Lifts Revenue Outlook After Data Center Orders Surge
summary: ABB Ltd. raised its revenue expectations for the year after a jump in orders for its power-grid products related to data centers.

title: AI Firms Seeking Office Space Boost London Developers’ Outlook
summary: London property developers are benefiting from the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence companies, driving demand for premium office and corporate campus space.

title: Memory Stock Valuations Spark Debate Over ‘Supercycle’ Potential
summary: Memory makers are riding surging demand to record profits, yet their stocks are still trading at a fraction of the valuation multiples of other top artificial intelligence chip names.

title: Deutsche Telekom Said to Weigh T-Mobile Combination
summary: Deutsche Telekom is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US, a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Anthropic’s Mythos Accessed by Unauthorized Users
summary: A small group of unauthorized users have accessed Anthropic's new Mythos AI model, a technology that the company says is so powerful it can enable dangerous cyberattacks, according to a person familiar with the matter and documentation viewed by Bloomberg News. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matt Bloxham explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: RBA, RBNZ Monitor Anthropic’s Mythos Over Cyberattack Fears
summary: Australia and New Zealand’s central banks are monitoring developments around Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model, which the company says is powerful enough to enable sophisticated cyberattacks, according to separate statements.

title: Crypto Billionaire Sues Trump-Linked Project Alleging Extortion
summary: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun and his companies sued World Liberty Financial, a crypto project co-founded by President Donald Trump, accusing the company of extortion and an “illegal scheme” to seize his tokens.

title: Upcoming AI Movie Sparks Concerns on Chinese Internet
summary: A proposed AI-generated movie based on the popular series "Soul Ferry" is trending on Weibo - and not everyone is happy. Bloomberg China Correspondent Minmin Low and Senior Analyst Robert Lea discuss the production’s implications and how AI could reshape companies like iQiyi. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Lifts Markets Past Iran War Shock, William Blair Says
summary: Vivian Lin Thurston, Portfolio Manager at William Blair, says the worst of the Iran war shock may be over as AI-driven earnings help global equities look past the turmoil. She speaks on Bloomberg's The China Show.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ASMPT Soars to Record as Sales Forecast Beat on AI Demand
summary: Chip gear maker ASMPT Ltd. shares jumped as much as 8.7% to a record as its second-quarter revenue beat expectations, driven by strong growth from its semiconductor business critical for AI.

title: Sinopec Cuts CATL Stake in Half After 180% Post-Listing Rally
summary: A unit of Sinopec, China’s biggest oil refiner, has cut its stake by more than half in battery maker Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. after a sharp rally in the stock.

title: SpaceX Has Deal for Right to Acquire Cursor for $60 Billion
summary: SpaceX said it has an agreement giving it the right to acquire artificial intelligence startup Cursor for $60 billion later this year or to pay $10 billion for the companies’ work together, part of the Elon Musk-run firm’s efforts to catch up with rivals in AI coding tools.

title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Plans Its First Data Center-Backed Bond
summary: Blackstone Inc.-owned data center operator AirTrunk Pte. is looking to raise at least A$500 million ($358 million) through asset-backed bonds, according to people familiar with the matter, in what may be one of Asia’s first in the sector.

title: Apple’s New CEO Will Need to Stave Off Exodus of Top Talent
summary: John Ternus, Apple Inc.’s next chief executive officer, is up against a major challenge as he settles into the role: retaining top talent during a time of historic turnover.

title: UK Says Iran, China Drive Regular Significant Cyberattacks
summary: Hackers linked to foreign governments are now behind the majority of “nationally significant” cyberattacks targeting the UK, the country’s top cybersecurity official is set to warn.

title: Italy’s Giorgetti Says Deficit Will Be Under EU Limit This Year
summary: Premier Giorgia Meloni’s government insisted Italy can still bring its deficit down to below the European Union’s ceiling this year, even if the economy performs worse than forecast.

title: Starmer’s Labour Set for ‘Historic Losses’ in London Local Vote
summary: Labour is set to hemorrhage votes to both the Greens on the left and Nigel Farage’s Reform UK on the right during local elections in London next month, according to a new poll that lays bare the flagging popularity of Keir Starmer’s governing party.

title: Germany Plans First Auction for New Gas Plants in September
summary: Germany plans to start holding tenders from September to build new gas-fired power plants, a draft law seen by Bloomberg shows, a move that has come under pressure from the chancellor’s junior coalition partner.

title: Turkey Says COP31 Climate Summit to Focus on Clean Energy Shift
summary: Turkey will use its role as host of this year’s COP31 climate summit to boost clean energy adoption, according to the event’s incoming president, with the Iran war showing how important it is to diversify away from fossil fuels.

title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Again, Amid Ship Seizures
summary: The holding pattern means markets are learning to live with uncertainty

title: Romanian President Holds Talks to Save Government From Collapse
summary: Romanian President Nicusor Dan is holding talks with coalition parties to salvage the country’s teetering pro-European government, with the nation’s investment grade and billions of euros in European Union funding potentially hinging on the outcome.

title: Ukraine to Get €90 Billion EU Loan After Hungary Drops Veto
summary: Ukraine will finally start receiving a €90 billion ($106 billion) loan from the European Union after Hungary lifted its veto, ending months of wrangling over the vital cash injection for the war-torn country.

title: US and Iran Deadlocked Over Hormuz After Trump Extends Truce
summary: US President Donald Trump indefinitely extended a ceasefire with Iran after the two sides failed to meet for more peace talks, with the war developing into a game of brinkmanship over the critical Strait of Hormuz.

title: Malacca Ship Tolls Divide Emerges Between Indonesia, Singapore
summary: Top officials from Indonesia and Singapore struck starkly different tones Wednesday on charging tolls in the Malacca Strait, a debate sparked by Iran’s push to levy fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Iran Gunboats Menace Hormuz as Its Own Tankers Test US Blockade
summary: Iranian gunboats fired on two ships in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday as Tehran maintained its grip on the vital energy channel, while two of its own oil supertankers tested a US blockade.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte’s Wealth Scrutinized in Impeachment Probe
summary: Philippine lawmakers expanded their impeachment probe into Vice President Sara Duterte to include her financial transactions, as they scrutinized why she didn’t report any cash holdings for six years.

title: War Crimes Court Rejects Bid to Free Philippines’ Duterte
summary: The International Criminal Court’s appeals chamber ruled that the tribunal has jurisdiction over the case of ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte’s crimes against humanity and rejected his bid for release.

title: Traders Bet on Post-War Calm to Keep Rates in Tight Range
summary: Traders are building up bets that bond market volatility will keep falling, even as a US-Iran peace agreement remains elusive.

title: MAHA’s Hopes for Healthier School Lunches Collide With Trump’s Spending Cuts
summary: “We’re managing pennies.” Cafeterias are being pushed to serve healthier meals with less money.

title: Starmer’s Uncivil War Against the UK Civil Service
summary: The Mandelson saga has laid bare tensions between the civil service and elected politicians.

title: German Defense Minister Expects FCAS Jet Decision This Week
summary: Germany expects a final decision this week on the future of a prestigious joint fighter jet project with France.

title: Oil Shock Pushes Thailand to Rework Budget to Hold Deficit
summary: Thailand is reshuffling state spending to cushion the impact of an oil shock while keeping its deficit target intact, highlighting the strain the Middle East conflict is placing on public finances.

title: Eskom Projects No Power Cuts This Winter, Warns of Lingering Risks
summary: South Africa’s state power utility said it should be able to avoid instituting electricity cuts this winter thanks to a marked improvement in the performance of its plants, while highlighting longer-term supply risks and mounting overdue debt as key concerns.

title: US-Iran Talks on Hold, Trump Comments on China’s ‘Gift’
summary: US President Donald Trump indefinitely extended a ceasefire with Iran as peace talks remain on hold, walking back threats to resume fighting even as the Strait of Hormuz remains all but shut. Separately, Trump made cryptic comments about China possibly providing weapons or other potentially lethal war supplies to Iran, a move that would test a US red line on aiding Tehran during the war. Bloomberg’s Joumanna Bercetche reports.

title: Noboa’s ‘New Ecuador’ Has Some Fearing Loss of Civil Freedoms
summary: On a recent Sunday morning, Ecuadorians awoke to find that the front pages of two widely circulated newspapers were blank.

title: Iranian Oil Tankers Sail Past Hormuz Blockade
summary: At least two fully laden Iranian tankers have sailed out of the Persian Gulf and past a US blockade this week, part of a flotilla that has made its way around the warships and ferried roughly 9 million barrels of oil to the market. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ships Come Under Gunfire Near Strait of Hormuz, UK Navy Says
summary: The UK Navy said two ships were fired at near the Strait of Hormuz, the latest incidents following shootings over the weekend that ratcheted tensions higher in the vital waterway and kept maritime traffic at a near standstill.

title: China Lauds ‘Relevant Nations’ Over Scrapped Taiwan Leader Trip
summary: China indicated it was pleased with three countries that apparently blocked Taiwan President Lai Ching-te’s trip to an African diplomatic ally, as Beijing seeks to further isolate the self-ruled democracy.

title: US-Iran Talks Stall, Ceasefire Extended
summary: While the US-Iran ceasefire extension announced by US President Donald Trump might ease tensions for now, the path toward a lasting agreement to end the war is uncertain. Bloomberg's Paul Wallace reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mandelson Fiasco Leaves Starmer Looking Like a Lame Duck
summary: Forty-eight hours of brutal public inquiry into Keir Starmer’s judgment have left the prime minister looking increasingly like a leader on his way out.

title: Ukraine Says It Asked Turkey to Help Seek Zelenskiy-Putin Talks
summary: Ukraine has asked Turkey to help arrange a summit meeting between President Volodymyr Zelenskiy and Russian leader Vladimir Putin in a renewed effort to end the war, Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said in Kyiv.

title: Iran War May Erase Most of Reeves’ Buffer, UK Study Says
summary: The war in Iran could wipe out most of Rachel Reeves’ fiscal buffer, according to new research pointing to a £16 billion ($22 billion) hit to Britain’s public finances.

title: UK’s Revered and Once Discreet Civil Service Turns on Starmer
summary: The UK’s Labour government roared to power promising to improve morale in a civil service degraded by budget cuts under 14 years of Conservative rule. Instead, a public spat with some of its most senior officials is hurting its ability to navigate war with its enemies and disagreements with its friends.

title: German Economy Chief to Make First Trip to China to Talk Trade
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is dispatching his economy minister to China next month as officials in Berlin grow increasingly concerned over a widening trade deficit, access to raw materials and regulation of artificial intelligence.
